title: dsDNA quantification using Sybr Green I
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"549la","text":"This is a simple protocol that uses Sybr Green 1 and a microplate reader to quantify dsDNA concentrations in unknown samples. This is best suited for situations where a high number of samples need to be quantified (e.g. normalisation of samples before pooling for Illumina sequencing). For smaller sample numbers (e.g. \u003c 50 samples) it will be more efficient to simply use a qubit or nanodrop.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7u5ts","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"52hv3","text":"You need to pick an appropriate standard concentration range for the type of samples you will be testing. For genomic DNA it is probably better to have a lower concentration range (0-10 ng/μl) whereas for PCR products, standards will probably need to be much more concentrated (0-50 or maybe even 100ng/μl).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Select which standards we need to make and use based on the strength of the samples being tested.

Serially dilute this as below: %s %s Dilution lambda DNA (µL) TE (µL) [lambda DNA] (ng/μl) 1 1000 (from 10 µg/mL stock) 0 10 2 500 (from dilution 1) 500 5 3 500 (from dilution 2) 500 2.5 4 500 (from dilution 3) 500 1.25 5 500 (from dilution 4) 500 0.625 6 500 (from dilution 5) 500 0.313 7 500 (from dilution 6) 500 0.156 8 0 (from dilution 7) 500 0.000
Using NEB lambda DNA (item N3011S) make a 100 ng/μl dsDNA standard in TE (100 μl of 500ng/μl lambda DNA + 400 μl of TE). TE =10 mM Tris HCI, 1 mM EDTA, pH 8

Serially dilute this as below: %s %s Dilution lambda DNA (µL) TE (µL) [lambda DNA] (ng/μl) 1 1000 (from 10 µg/mL stock) 0 10 2 500 (from dilution 1) 500 5 3 500 (from dilution 2) 500 2.5 4 500 (from dilution 3) 500 1.25 5 500 (from dilution 4) 500 0.625 6 500 (from dilution 5) 500 0.313 7 500 (from dilution 6) 500 0.156 8 0 (from dilution 7) 500 0.000
Aliquot 20 μl of each of these into PCR strips and store at -20 °c until needed. Thaw one for each run on the plate reader. If you have previously made your dilution series start at this point.

Make a 100x Sybr Green 1 solution (5ul Sybr Green 1 + 495 μl of TE). This can be pipetted out into 50 - 100 μl aliquots and stored at -20 °c. Once thawed, do not reuse. 96 μl of 100x Sybr Green 1 is required per plate of samples. If you have previously made your 100X Sybr Green 1 start at this point.

Make a solution of 1x Syber Green 1 as below: A B C 1 Volume for one sample Volume for n + 136 samples 2 TE 97 3 100X Sybr Green 1 1 4 Total 98 Table 2: Illustrative table for calculating the dilution to make enough 1x Sybr Green 1 for n samples

Distribute 98 μl of Sybr Green 1 working solution into the first 4 columns of a Nunc black microtiter plate and 100 μl into the last 8 columns. This is best done using a multichannel electronic repeater or 96 well pipette if you are testing a lot of samples.
Distribute 98 μl of sybr green 1 working solution into n wells of a nunc black microtiter plate. Use multiple plates for more than 96 samples.
Add 2 μl of sample dsDNA into each of the sample wells.
Seal with a clear plate seal.
Incubate at room temperature in the dark for 10 minutes. 00:10:00
Run samples on your plate reader according to manufacturers instructions with an excitation wavelength of 485 nM and an emission wavelength of 535 nM.

Check that your calibration curve has a very high R 2 value (greater than 0.95) and multiply your measured concentrations by 2.5 to get a final concentration. Figure 2: A typical calibration curve from the above protocol
Using NEB lambda DNA (item N3011S) make a 10 ng/μl dsDNA standard in TE (20ul of 500ng/μl lambda DNA + 980 μl of TE). TE =10 mM Tris HCI, 1 mM EDTA, pH 8
Aliquot 20 μl of each of these into PCR strips and store at -20 °c until needed. Thaw one for each run on the plate reader. If you have previously made your dilution series start at this point.

Make a 100x Sybr Green 1 solution (5ul Sybr Green 1 + 495 μl of TE). This can be pipetted out into 50 - 100 μl aliquots and stored at -20 °c. Once thawed, do not reuse. 96 μl of 100x Sybr Green 1 is required per plate of samples. If you have previously made your 100X Sybr Green 1 start at this point.

Make a solution of 1x Syber Green 1 as below: A B C 1 Volume for one sample Volume for n + 136 samples 2 TE 97 3 100X Sybr Green 1 1 4 Total 98 Table 2: Illustrative table for calculating the dilution to make enough 1x Sybr Green 1 for n samples

Distribute 98 μl of Sybr Green 1 working solution into the first 4 columns of a Nunc black microtiter plate and 100 μl into the last 8 columns. This is best done using a multichannel electronic repeater or 96 well pipette if you are testing a lot of samples.

Using a p10 multichannel pipette, add 2ul of each standard into the first 4 columns of the control plate as shown in figure 1. General control plate layout Pipetting accuracy is key here. Accurate standards will greatly improve the accuracy of the samples. You can increase the number of standard replicates if you wish.
Distribute 98 μl of sybr green 1 working solution into n wells of a nunc black microtiter plate. Use multiple plates for more than 96 samples.
Add 2 μl of sample dsDNA into each of the sample wells.
Seal with a clear plate seal.
Incubate at room temperature in the dark for 10 minutes. 00:10:00
Run samples on your plate reader according to manufacturers instructions with an excitation wavelength of 485 nM and an emission wavelength of 535 nM.

Check that your calibration curve has a very high R 2 value (greater than 0.95) and multiply your measured concentrations by 2.5 to get a final concentration. Figure 2: A typical calibration curve from the above protocol

Using a p10 multichannel pipette, add 2ul of each standard into the first 4 columns of the control plate as shown in figure 1. General control plate layout Pipetting accuracy is key here. Accurate standards will greatly improve the accuracy of the samples. You can increase the number of standard replicates if you wish.
